title: Cynoglossum enerve
taxonomy: Cynoglossum enerve is classified within the plant family Boraginaceae. This family comprises a large number of flowering plant species commonly known as borage or forget-me-not. Cynoglossum enerve belongs to the genus Cynoglossum, which includes a variety of species often referred to as hound's-tongue. The species is native to various regions, including North America. Its classification as a member of the Boraginaceae family is consistent with its botanical characteristics and growth patterns.

intro: Cynoglossum nervosum, also known as the Nervous Hound's Tongue or Nervous Glossum, is a perennial plant species belonging to the borage family (Boraginaceae). This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including anxiety, insomnia, and inflammation, due to its sedative and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Cynoglossum nervosum include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Cynoglossum nervosum include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
